name: stream
context: Streaming chat completions via Server-Sent Events for real-time token-by-token output
info: Streaming chat completions deliver tokens incrementally via Server-Sent Events (SSE) as the model generates them. Set `stream: true` in the request to `POST /v1/chat/completions` and receive a stream of `data:` events, each containing a chunk with `choices[0].delta.content`. This reduces time-to-first-token dramatically — the client sees output within milliseconds instead of waiting seconds for the full response. The Node.js SDK returns an async iterable; iterate with `for await (const chunk of stream)`. Streaming is essential for chat UIs, CLIs with live output, and any latency-sensitive application. The final chunk contains `finish_reason` indicating completion. Token usage is available in the final chunk when `stream_options: { include_usage: true }` is set.

sections.title: Delta format
sections.body: Each streamed chunk contains `choices[0].delta` instead of `choices[0].message`. The first chunk has `delta.role` ("assistant"). Subsequent chunks have `delta.content` with incremental text. The final chunk has an empty `delta` and `finish_reason` set to `stop`, `length`, `tool_calls`, or `content_filter`. Tool call deltas arrive as `delta.tool_calls` with incremental function argument JSON.

sections.title: Usage tracking in streams
sections.body: By default, streaming does not include usage stats. Set `stream_options: { include_usage: true }` to receive a final chunk with `usage` containing `prompt_tokens`, `completion_tokens`, and `total_tokens`. This final usage chunk has empty choices. Always check `chunk.usage` to detect the usage event.

sections.title: Stream error handling
sections.body: Stream errors can occur mid-generation. The SDK throws on connection errors, timeout, and server errors. Wrap the `for await` loop in a try/catch. Use AbortController to cancel long-running streams. On 429 rate limit errors, the SDK retries automatically before the stream starts, but errors during an active stream are not retried. See [[openai/rate-limits]] for backoff strategies.

related.note: Use non-streaming for simpler integration when latency is not critical
Assistants API also supports streaming runs via runs.stream()
